subtopic_name: Programmatic stock research with the Claude Agent SDK
summary: The Claude Agent SDK (renamed from the Claude Code SDK) is the official Python and TypeScript library that exposes the same agent loop, built-in tools, and context management that powers Claude Code, suitable for orchestrating stock-research workflows. Developers use query() for one-shot async iteration or ClaudeSDKClient for bidirectional sessions, configured by ClaudeAgentOptions (model, allowed_tools, system_prompt, hooks, MCP servers, permission_mode). Custom research tools (e.g., a TSLA price fetcher, SEC filing parser, news scraper) are defined with the @tool decorator and bundled into in-process MCP servers via create_sdk_mcp_server. Streaming, prompt caching, sub-agents, and headless CI runs are first-class.

key_claims.claim: The Claude Agent SDK ships official libraries for Python (claude-agent-sdk on PyPI) and TypeScript (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk on npm), giving the same Claude Code agent loop programmatic access.

query() returns an async iterator for single-shot agent runs, while ClaudeSDKClient supports bidirectional multi-turn conversations and is the only path that enables custom in-process tools and hooks.

Custom research tools are declared with the @tool decorator and packaged with create_sdk_mcp_server into an in-process MCP server, then exposed through ClaudeAgentOptions.mcp_servers and gated via allowed_tools entries like mcp__tools__greet.

Streaming is opt-in via include_partial_messages (Python) or includePartialMessages (TypeScript); the SDK then emits StreamEvent messages wrapping raw Claude API events such as content_block_delta with text_delta or input_json_delta chunks.
Prompt caching uses cache_control breakpoints with two TTLs: 5-minute writes cost 1.25x the base input price and 1-hour writes cost 2x, while cache reads cost 0.1x of base input regardless of TTL.
A request may set up to 4 explicit cache_control breakpoints, and the system looks back 20 blocks for prior cache entries before giving up.
Minimum cacheable prefix is model-dependent: 4096 tokens for Opus 4.7/4.6/4.5 and Haiku 4.5, 2048 tokens for Sonnet 4.6, 1024 tokens for older Sonnet 4.x.
Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, and Sonnet 4.6 all expose a 1M-token context window at standard per-token pricing, which fits multi-quarter TSLA filings plus news corpora in a single agent context.
Opus 4.7 (model id claude-opus-4-7) requires Agent SDK v0.2.111 or later; older SDK versions surface a thinking.type.enabled API error.

The SDK supports headless and CI/CD usage and authenticates via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or third-party providers (Bedrock via CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, Vertex via CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, Azure via CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY).

Hook callbacks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit) let a research pipeline log every tool call (e.g., audit each WebFetch of a TSLA filing) or block dangerous shell commands deterministically.

Sub-agents defined via AgentDefinition (Python) or the agents option (TypeScript) let a top-level research agent delegate focused subtasks (e.g., a 'sec-filings-reader' or 'price-analyst') with their own tool allowlists.
External MCP servers (e.g., Playwright for headless browser scraping of Yahoo Finance) plug in via mcp_servers with stdio command and args, alongside SDK in-process servers.
The Batch API offers a 50% discount on both input and output tokens and stacks with prompt caching multipliers, useful for overnight backfills of TSLA-related research jobs.

limitations: The npm registry page returned HTTP 403 to the fetcher, so the TypeScript version number was confirmed only via the GitHub repo page rather than the npm listing; weekly download counts could not be retrieved.
